rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Walk-away test (M2+) — student leaves the chair while the agent works
50% magic rule (M3–M8)
Trust the agentic design — no human backstops
Exercise quality ladder — target 8, design 9 for peaks
One exercise = one bounded activity
Student agency — adults decide; opportunity ≠ compulsion
Delegation boundaries — student brings, Claude screens
Artifact-realism check — named tools/skills/files must exist and be available
Auto-fire sim + eval after significant rewrite
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 85-86: 'draft 2026-04-29 (organisers-only rollout synthesis split out; sim/mechanical/eval not rerun)' — maintainer block explicitly notes sim/mechanical/eval not rerun after the structural split.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Run sim + eval after the 2026-04-29 split; quality state is draft and no eval has been run.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions stay, scripted reactions go — live in prompts not body prose
Corporate infra is nice-to-have, never a forcing function
Personal-first, team promotion via human conversation
Reflection beats prefer in-chat over file-write
Classroom default; body teaches the work, trainer manages the room — no conversations in body
Audience tier determines authoring substrate
Plug points + UX — the file must be both plug-able and operable
Per-exercise leap test — name three observable Monday-morning outcomes
Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch for every forcing function
Heavy fan-out prompts get a body-side recovery callout
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 37: Recovery nudges ('check again', 'Show me the readout again') appear after the Phase 1 prompt but do not name that a heavy/long-running continuous-poll loop is expected, nor provide an interrupt+steer callout for when the agent over-reads.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a pre-prompt body callout before the Phase 1 fence naming (a) the continuous-polling loop is expected to run through several group file appearances, and (b) the recovery move when the loop over-reads — interrupt, steer narrower, or say 'continue'.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Convenience-driven prompts with known shape-bias get body-side acknowledgment callouts
Session widget — source shape and placement by learning flow
Pre-action framing blocks don't preview the prompt's payoff
Multi-session ships as packaging, not as a new taught primitive
/clear is a tool with a purpose, not a ceremony
